texto: There seems to be a different Chicago around every street corner, behind every bar, and within every apartment, two-flat, cottage, or bungalow. City of immigrants or city of heartless plutocrats, say what you will, Chicago almost defies interpretation. In many ways Chicago is like a snake that sheds its skin every thirty years or so and puts on a new coat to conform to a new reality.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dominic A. Pacyga: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “There seems to be a different Chicago around every street corner, behind every bar, and within ev…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, chicago, city pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e city.
